Ghost versions fix · fix/pre-push-ghost-versions

Closes #1457.

The bug

Two manual bump-version.sh minor calls in the last 72 hours created tag-less version numbers:

v7.65.0   ← tagged (just now)
v7.63.1   ← tagged
v7.63.0   ← tagged
v7.61.0   ← tagged
v7.62.0   ← GHOST — my bump, release-please picked 7.63.0
v7.64.0   ← GHOST — my bump, release-please picked 7.65.0
v7.60.0   ← tagged

Root cause

Pre-push hook enforces manual version bumps on feat/fix/perf/refactor/* branches. Release-please (release-type: simple) computes its own next version from conventional-commit types since last tag and overwrites whatever the manual bump produced. When they disagree, the manually-typed version becomes a ghost.

The fix

One-line change in bin/git-hooks/pre-push:

- if [[ "$BRANCH" =~ ^(docs|chore|ci|style|test)/ ]]; then
+ if [[ "$BRANCH" =~ ^(docs|chore|ci|style|test|feat|fix|perf|refactor)/ ]]; then
    echo "  ✓ Skipping version check for $BRANCH"
    exit 0
  fi

Release-please owns version decisions on all conventional-commit branches. The pre-push check added no value on those branches and actively caused ghosts.

Meta validation

This fix's own branch — fix/pre-push-ghost-versions — matches the new skip pattern. The pre-push hook did NOT demand a version bump. The fix works on itself. 🪞

Also shipped

CONTRIBUTING.md gains a "Versioning" section documenting:

  • Don't manually run bump-version.sh on conventional-commit branches
  • Use release-please's release-as commit footer if you need to override its computed next
  • Hotfix branches with bare names still trigger the check (for legitimate manual control)

Install note

git config core.hooksPath = bin/git-hooks is already set. .git/hooks/pre-push is a symlink. Change is live without a separate install step.

Expected outcome

Next feat/* or fix/* PR to main: pre-push runs, sees branch prefix in the new skip list, exits 0. Release-please opens a PR with its computed version, no ghost number.
